@@ -607,6 +607,11 @@
 {"id":"frankenredis-r73v","title":"[HIGH] RDB+AOF persistence round-trip parity: load upstream-produced .rdb + .aof and re-emit byte-identical on save","description":"Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 3 (cross-impl round-trip) to RDB + AOF persistence:

(a) Load a corpus of upstream-produced dump.rdb files covering every type tag we emit (string, list_quicklist_2, set/intset/listpack, hash/listpack, zset_2/listpack, stream_listpacks_3, plus 7.4 hash_metadata family). Verify each loads cleanly and matches the in-memory state upstream would produce. Resave; feed back to upstream redis-server --loadrdb; verify XINFO STREAM FULL / HGETALL / etc. parity.
(b) Same for AOF: load upstream-produced appendonly.aof files (with RDB-preamble + multi-DB SELECT + EXPIREAT + complex transactions), verify replay + resave path is byte-stable.
(c) Re-encode our RDB with --rdbcompression yes (upstream's default) and verify upstream loads the LZF-encoded strings. Today we never emit LZF on save.
(d) Verify the RDB version header (REDIS0011 for 7.2, REDIS0012 for 7.4) matches what we negotiate.

The DUMP/RESTORE round-trip we just landed (br-frankenredis-v8s5) is at the wire level for single keys; this bead is the FILE level for full snapshots.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aof","persistence","rdb","roundtrip"]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-r74v","title":"[HIGH] Pub/Sub scalability + keyspace notifications: SSUBSCRIBE/SPUBLISH (sharded), CLUSTER PUBSUB SHARDCHANNELS, K/E notify masks","description":"(a) Sharded pub/sub (Redis 7+): SSUBSCRIBE/SUNSUBSCRIBE/SPUBLISH against shard-channels. CLUSTER PUBSUB SHARDCHANNELS / SHARDNUMSUB introspection. Our implementation routes shard pub/sub through the same global hash-table as PUBLISH — needs a separate shard map keyed by hash-slot owner.
(b) Keyspace notifications: CONFIG SET notify-keyspace-events 'KEA' should turn on __keyspace@<db>__:<key> and __keyevent@<db>__:<event> message generation for every applicable command. Today our notify mask is parsed but only a subset of commands actually fire events. Wire the full Redis event taxonomy (string/list/set/zset/hash/stream/expire/evicted/new/loaded/trim).
(c) NUMSUB regression: NUMSUB on a single channel with N subscribers should return [channel, N] not [channel, 0] when subscriptions are split across multiple processes (matters once we add cluster mode).
(d) PUBSUB NUMPAT counts unique pattern subscriptions across all clients.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 6: drive scenarios from a multi-client harness (extends fr_p2c_006 replication journey infrastructure).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["keyspace-notifications","pubsub","scalability"]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-r75v","title":"[HIGH] Lua scripting parity audit: EVAL/EVALSHA/EVAL_RO + FUNCTION CREATE/LOAD/CALL/FLUSH/DUMP/RESTORE + script-side redis.* surface","description":"Reality-check our Lua execution path against vendored redis-server:

(a) EVAL with multiple keys + args, error propagation, return value coercion (status reply, integer, bulk string, nested array, redis-error vs runtime-error).
(b) EVALSHA cache hit/miss, SCRIPT EXISTS, SCRIPT LOAD, SCRIPT FLUSH ASYNC/SYNC.
(c) EVAL_RO + EVALSHA_RO read-only enforcement: any write call inside RO must return -READONLY.
(d) FUNCTION LOAD with the !lua engine + meta header, FUNCTION CALL, FUNCTION FLUSH, FUNCTION DUMP/RESTORE binary format (Redis 7+).
(e) The redis.* surface inside scripts: redis.call, redis.pcall, redis.error_reply, redis.status_reply, redis.sha1hex, redis.log, redis.replicate_commands(), redis.breakpoint(), redis.debug(), redis.set_repl(), KEYS / ARGV indexing semantics.
(f) MULTI inside EVAL: should be rejected; EXEC inside EVAL should error.
(g) FCALL_RO + script-flag #!lua flags=no-writes / allow-stale / allow-oom / allow-cross-slot-keys.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: extend core_scripting.json + core_function.json with each combination, run live-oracle diff.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["eval","function","lua","scripting"]}
+{"id":"frankenredis-r80v","title":"[HIGH] RESP3 push frame routing: pub/sub messages, client-side caching invalidations, tracking redirects must use '>' framing on RESP3 sessions","description":"After HELLO 3, upstream Redis switches asynchronous-server-message paths from RESP2 Array to RESP3 Push frame ('>' prefix per https://github.com/redis/redis-specifications/blob/master/protocol/RESP3.md#push-type). Today our runtime continues to emit Arrays:

(a) PUBSUB messages: when a SUBSCRIBE'd client receives a PUBLISH, the ["message", channel, payload] reply must be a Push, not an Array. Same for ["smessage", ...] (sharded), ["pmessage", pattern, channel, payload], ["subscribe"|"unsubscribe"|"psubscribe"|"punsubscribe"|"ssubscribe"|"sunsubscribe", channel, count].

(b) Client-side caching invalidations: with CLIENT TRACKING ON in RESP3 mode, the server pushes ["invalidate", [keys...]] frames to the tracking redirect target on key mutation. Today our runtime queues these on the wrong stream and as Array.

(c) Server-side notifications (when more land): keyspace notifications via CONFIG SET notify-keyspace-events route through the same pub/sub layer; verify the push framing follows.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: extend the live-oracle harness to negotiate HELLO 3 on the dedicated connection used for these cases, then assert the wire frame on the receiving side starts with the '>' byte under RESP3 (and '*' under RESP2).

Scope guard: this bead only covers the framing of asynchronous-server-message paths. Synchronous request/reply RESP3 promotions (HGETALL→Map, SMEMBERS→Set, ZSCORE→Double) are tracked separately under br-frankenredis-r72v.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenredis","created_at":"2026-04-25T03:30:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-25T05:15:56.813244354Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T05:15:56.811371439Z","close_reason":"Completed in 5c83cec","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["client-tracking","pubsub","push","resp3"]}
+{"id":"frankenredis-r81v","title":"[HIGH] ACL category-level grants: +@write/-@admin/+@dangerous interaction with explicit +cmd/-cmd, GETUSER selectors structure","description":"Audit ACL category-grant precedence + selector reply shape against vendored redis-server:

(a) When a user has rule `+@all -@dangerous +flushdb`, the explicit +flushdb must override the -@dangerous deny because explicit per-command rules win over category rules. Verify our matcher does that and not the other order.

(b) `-@all +@read +set` should allow SET (explicit grant beats -@all category) and reject everything else not in @read. Verify GET/SET/DEL.

(c) GETUSER selectors[] field (Redis 7.0+ multi-selector support): the user can have N selectors each with their own keys/channels/commands; reply shape is an array of selector maps. Today we emit an empty array; verify what upstream emits for a user with zero selectors (single empty-or-omitted shape) vs ≥1 selectors (array of selector dicts).

(d) ACL SETUSER `(channel-pattern command-list)` syntax: parses a selector. Verify our SETUSER accepts the `(...)` selector group and adds it to the user's selectors[] vector; reject malformed selectors with upstream wording.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: extend core_acl.json with these cases and run live-oracle diff.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-25T03:30:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-25T03:30:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["acl","category","precedence","selectors"]}
+{"id":"frankenredis-r82v","title":"[HIGH] XAUTOCLAIM semantics: cursor cycling, JUSTID, COUNT, MINIDLETIME, deleted-entries reply slot (Redis 7+)","description":"XAUTOCLAIM is the Redis 7.0+ replacement for XCLAIM in idempotent retry loops. Reply shape:
  [next_cursor_id, claimed_entries_array, deleted_entries_array]

Verify against vendored redis-server:

(a) Cursor semantics: the returned next_cursor_id is the next ID to scan from on the next XAUTOCLAIM invocation; '0-0' means the iteration is complete.

(b) JUSTID option: returns just IDs (no field/value pairs).

(c) COUNT N: limits the batch.

(d) MINIDLETIME ms: only claim entries whose idle time >= ms.

(e) Deleted entries: when a pending entry was XDEL'd, it appears in the third reply slot (id-only, never the second slot).

(f) Group/consumer creation: if the consumer doesn't exist, it is created automatically (parity with XCLAIM).

(g) Error wording: NOGROUP for missing group, wrong-arity wording for short argv.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: extend core_stream.json with XAUTOCLAIM cases (with PEL setup, idle-time advancement, JUSTID toggles, deleted-entry intermixing) and run live-oracle diff.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-25T03:30:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-25T03:30:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["consumer-groups","pel","streams","xautoclaim"]}
+{"id":"frankenredis-r83v","title":"[HIGH] FUNCTION DUMP/RESTORE binary format (Redis 7+): library serialization, FLUSH ASYNC|SYNC, REPLACE flag","description":"Redis 7+ adds FUNCTION DUMP / FUNCTION RESTORE for library transfer between servers. Format:
  payload = type_byte(1) + library_blob + version_u16_le + crc64_le
The library_blob is a length-prefixed engine-specific (lua) bytestream containing:
  [meta_header (#!lua name=<lib> [flags=...]) + function_count + per-function (name, body, flags, sha1)]

Verify against vendored redis-server:

(a) FUNCTION DUMP returns a BulkString with the documented envelope; CRC64 must match Redis crc64.

(b) FUNCTION RESTORE accepts our DUMP output (round-trip), and we accept upstream's DUMP output. Cross-impl pattern as with v8s5 STRING/STREAM.

(c) FUNCTION RESTORE with REPLACE replaces an existing library; without REPLACE on an existing library yields the upstream error.

(d) FUNCTION RESTORE with FLUSH first clears the function space then loads.

(e) FUNCTION FLUSH ASYNC|SYNC accepts both modifiers; default is SYNC. Wrong modifier returns upstream syntax error.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 3 (cross-impl round-trip): extend core_function.json with DUMP/RESTORE cases, plus a new live_redis_function_dump_restore_roundtrip_matches_upstream test mirroring the STRING/STREAM/container round-trip gates.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-25T03:30:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-25T03:30:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dump-restore","function","lua","roundtrip"]}
+{"id":"frankenredis-r84v","title":"[HIGH] CLUSTER shard map negotiation: SHARDS reply slot ranges + node addr/port/id, MEET/FORGET edge cases, COUNT-FAILURE-REPORTS shape","description":"Even with cluster mode disabled, several CLUSTER subcommands need their reply shape verified against vendored redis-server (which we can spawn as a single-node cluster via cluster-enabled yes + a minimal nodes.conf).

(a) CLUSTER SHARDS reply: an array of shard maps. Each map has {slots: [start, end, ...], nodes: [{id, port, ip, role, replication-offset, health}]}. We model this as a flat array of pairs but the per-node sub-map shape needs verification.

(b) CLUSTER MEET <ip> <port> [<bus-port>]: rejects malformed ip/port with upstream wording. Verify error message for bus-port out of range, ip parse failure.

(c) CLUSTER FORGET <node-id>: error wording when node-id is unknown vs. when node is the current node ('I tried hard but I can't forget myself...').

(d) CLUSTER COUNT-FAILURE-REPORTS <node-id>: returns Integer 0 for unknown node (Redis 7+ behavior; earlier versions errored). Today we error.

(e) CLUSTER LINKS reply: array of link maps with direction, peer-id, send-buffer-allocated, etc.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1 + a single-node cluster bring-up step in the live-oracle harness.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenredis","created_at":"2026-04-25T03:30:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-25T06:33:49.314990074Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T06:33:49.312431992Z","close_reason":"Completed in 8356caa","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cluster","failure-reports","forget","meet","shards"]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-r8q","title":"Repair LuaTable hash access regression under strict clippy","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-19T04:05:38.824707277Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T04:33:19.482682767Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T04:33:19.482007944Z","close_reason":"Already fixed: LuaTable hash→string_hash/other_hash migration completed with hash_pairs() and hash_is_empty() helpers in commit 29f6f2c","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-r9h9","title":"redis parity: RESET and QUIT should reject script context","description":"Vendored Redis marks RESET and QUIT as NOSCRIPT in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/reset.json and quit.json, but the local handlers at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:13933 and crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:8683 return success without any script_nesting_level guard. Add Redis-compatible NOSCRIPT rejection after arity validation and cover both commands from Lua.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:07:11.688421378Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T15:26:35.234727873Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T15:26:35.232688321Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-ra8a","title":"redis parity: SUBSCRIBE/UNSUBSCRIBE/PSUBSCRIBE/PUNSUBSCRIBE/SSUBSCRIBE/SUNSUBSCRIBE should reject script context","description":"Vendored Redis marks SUBSCRIBE, UNSUBSCRIBE, PSUBSCRIBE, PUNSUBSCRIBE, SSUBSCRIBE, and SUNSUBSCRIBE as NOSCRIPT in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/subscribe.json, unsubscribe.json, psubscribe.json, punsubscribe.json, ssubscribe.json, and sunsubscribe.json. The handlers at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:12025, 12051, 12100, 12124, 6507, and 6532 have no script_nesting_level guard today. Add Redis-compatible NOSCRIPT handling for these subscription-mode commands and cover the single- and multi-argument forms from Lua.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:07:34.461651152Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:32:27.607008462Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T04:32:27.605084094Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
